hypothesis: Sweeping semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight itself (the lever E621's instrumentation showed actually drives target selection), with its sibling semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight fixed at 0 (the true, unconfounded control -- not E621's own swept arm, which held item_margin fixed nonzero), will show a real dose-response effect on OOD quality metrics.
falsifier: All dose levels (0, 1, 2, 4) produce byte-identical predictions across all seeds/records, matching E621's null.
method: Reused E621's 3 fresh 800-step scratch twotower checkpoints (seeds 0/1/2; e621-margin-sweep-seed{0,1,2}-scratch800-20260720, unchanged on disk) and replayed the same E619/E620/E621 standard eval recipe (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=8, semantic_role_decode_weight=8, slot_coverage_close_decode_weight=2, schema_value_decode_weight=4, schema_opaque_close_decode_weight=4, semantic_plan_decode_weight=4, semantic_plan_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_binding_decode_weight=1, semantic_plan_root_decode_weight=8, semantic_plan_root_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_array_close_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_slot_margin_decode_weight=2, honest_slot_contract/slot_contract_constrained_decode/slot_contract_in_context/semantic_role_contract_in_context/semantic_role_schema_candidates=true, grammar_ltr_max_tokens=160), holding semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight FIXED AT 0 (the true ablated-sibling control, per E621's own next-step recommendation (b)) and sweeping ONLY semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight at 0, 1, 2, 4. Ran 8 new real OOD n=4 eval runs (seed1/seed2 @ item=0; seed0/1/2 @ item=1; seed0/1/2 @ item=4) and reused 4 already-committed E621 runs unmodified: seed0 item=0 (=e621-seed0-bothzero-r1, both weights already 0) and seed0/1/2 item=2 (=e621-seed{0,1,2}-margin0-r1, nonempty already 0, item already fixed at the standard 2). 12 seed x dose combinations x n=4 OOD records = 48 real predictions total. A same-config sanity check (fresh seed0 item=0 rerun vs the reused e621-seed0-bothzero-r1 record) reproduced byte-identical placeholder_fidelity/validity/structural_similarity/reward_score/meaningful_program_rate, confirming the reuse is valid.

checkpoints_reused_unchanged.note: No new training this iteration -- pure decode-time re-evaluation of already-committed-evidence checkpoints from E621, same session-branch lineage.

eval_recipe.matched_to: E619/E620/E621's standard evaluation_policy, with semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight now held at 0 (the unconfounded control E621 identified as missing) and semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight swept instead

sanity_check_reproduction.result: byte-identical placeholder_fidelity (0.4667), placeholder_validity (0.58), structural_similarity (0.48855), reward_score (0.62675), meaningful_program_rate (0.5) vs e621-seed0-bothzero-r1 -- confirms determinism and that reusing E621's committed runs as data points is valid.
sanity_check_reproduction.note: This run is kept on disk (outputs/runs/e622-seed0-item0-r1/) but its metrics are not double-counted in the results table below; e621-seed0-bothzero-r1 is used as the seed0/item=0 data point.

byte_identical_predictions_check.method: prediction_sha256 field emitted per-record by evaluate_model, compared across all 4 doses within each seed/record
byte_identical_predictions_check.result: 9 of 12 seed x record groups (ood_auth_01, ood_dashboard_01, ood_modal_01 x 3 seeds = 36 of 48 predictions) are byte-identical across ALL FOUR doses (0/1/2/4). The remaining 3 groups (ood_gallery_01 x 3 seeds) show exactly 2 distinct values each: a unique value at item_margin=0, and one shared value across item_margin in {1, 2, 4} (byte-identical among those three). Only 3 of 48 predictions (the item_margin=0 gallery ones) differ from their group's positive-dose value.
byte_identical_predictions_check.gallery_prediction_sha256_at_item0_all_s: 591d4f88... (identical across seed 0/1/2 -- deterministic close-empty completion independent of checkpoint)

power_protocol_analysis.method_ref: slm_training.evals.power_protocol (wilson_interval, bootstrap_paired_ci, intraclass_correlation) -- same primitives E621 first applied to real multi-seed eval records
power_protocol_analysis.pooled_meaningful_v1_note: meaningful_program_v1 (binary pass/fail) does not flip for the gallery record at either state in this checkpoint scale, so the pooled binary rate is unchanged across all doses -- the effect only shows up in continuous metrics (fidelity/validity/reward).

paired_bootstrap_delta_fidelity_item1_mi.note: 12 paired seed x record fidelity observations; positive point estimate, CI touches 0 at the lower bound (small n=12), consistent with a real but modest per-record effect concentrated in 3/12 pairs.

paired_bootstrap_delta_fidelity_item2_mi.note: degenerate [0,0] -- every one of the 12 paired records is byte-identical between item_margin=1 and item_margin=2.

paired_bootstrap_delta_fidelity_item4_mi.note: degenerate [0,0] -- every one of the 12 paired records is byte-identical between item_margin=2 and item_margin=4.

icc_meaningful_v1_by_seed_at_item1.note: same small-sample estimation artifact E621 documented; not over-interpreted.

power_protocol_analysis.seed_variance_note: As in E621, seed dominates: reward_score at item_margin=0 ranges 0.2432 (seed2) to 0.6502 (seed1); the item_margin effect (a flat +0.17 to +0.20 reward shift per seed) rides on top of, and is much smaller than, seed-to-seed variance.

decision: Genuine, code-confirmed dose-response finding, but a MINIMUM-EFFECTIVE-DOSE threshold, not a monotonic scaling effect: semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight only needs to be POSITIVE to fully flip the one decode site it controls (ImageGallery's typed-array-open bias) at this checkpoint scale -- item_margin=1 is exactly as effective as item_margin=2 (the current standard-recipe default) or item_margin=4. There is zero marginal benefit from the recipe's current default of 2 over the untested lower value of 1 in this diagnostic; there is a real, measurable difference between 0 (off) and any positive dose (fidelity +0.083 to +0.125, validity +0.15 to +0.175, reward +0.187 to +0.200 across the 3 seeds, concentrated entirely in the one record whose item array this lever governs). Structural_similarity/AST F1/component_type_recall/meaningful_program_v1/strict_v2 are unchanged at every dose, matching E621's ablation. This completes E621's causal chain end-to-end (code read -> instrumented single firing site -> now dose-swept the correct lever with a clean unconfounded control) rather than leaving it as a hypothesis. No checkpoint promoted or synced; no code changed (verify_version_stamps --check: 0 components touched). Not a ship claim.
honest_caveats: n=4-per-arm, 3-seed diagnostic replay against ood only, not a full held-out/rico_held confirmatory suite.

The effect is driven by exactly one of the four OOD records (ood_gallery_01) at this checkpoint scale; the other three records show zero sensitivity to this lever across the entire 0-4 dose range tested. A checkpoint/prompt mix with more typed-array components could reveal additional firing sites and a different saturation point.

Only doses {0, 1, 2, 4} were tested; whether a fractional dose between 0 and 1 (e.g. 0.5) would show a partial/graded effect, or whether the threshold is a hard step at any positive value, is not established -- the code path (`if typed_margin > 0.0`) is a hard boolean gate, so a step function at exactly 0 is the code-predicted behavior and this is consistent with, not merely correlated with, that reading.
binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict (strict v2) stays 0.0 in every arm across all 3 seeds and all 4 doses -- required-slot coverage remains a fully open problem this iteration does not touch.

The negative ICC reported here is the same small-sample estimation artifact E621 documented (within-seed variance exceeding between-seed variance at n=3 clusters), not evidence of a real negative seed-cluster correlation.
next_step: E620's still-open findings (ood_dashboard_01's missing components/slots, ood_auth_01's Input.name role swap, ood_modal_01's undertrained structure) remain unaddressed by both E621 and E622 and are the strongest remaining candidate for a root-cause trace, since none of the margin-weight sweeps in this lineage (E621, E622) touch those three records at all -- they were byte-identical across every dose and weight combination tested so far in both iterations. Separately, since item_margin=1 is now shown to be exactly as effective as the standard recipe's default of 2 in this diagnostic, a future iteration could test whether shrinking the standard-recipe default from 2 to 1 has any effect on other suites/records where headroom (not just threshold-crossing) might matter, before treating margin=1 as a safe simplification.
test_plan: 8 new real matched OOD n=4 eval runs on already-trained E621 checkpoints (seeds 0/1/2 x doses {0,1,4}, minus the 4 already-committed E621 data points reused for doses {0 seed0, 2 all seeds})
1 same-config sanity-check rerun (seed0 item=0) confirming byte-identical reproduction of e621-seed0-bothzero-r1
python -m scripts.verify_version_stamps --check -> ok, 0 components touched (no source changed)
